figures.caption: Table 2: 2AFC perceptual evaluation (3,686 trials, 26 participants). Win% = fraction of trials in which the method was preferred.
Fig. 1: Pipeline overview: image acquisition, SAM segmentation, Hi3DGen mesh generation, expert refinement, and AI-assisted texturing.

Fig. 2: Qualitative comparison on a Vatican figure (bishop from Decretum Gratiani). Hi3DGen best preserves surface detail; Wonder3D produces the most volumetric but topologically flawed geometry; SAM 3D produces clean watertight meshes but almost flat.
Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison on a Monteprandone figure. SF3D and SPAR3D produce the closest silhouette match; Hi3DGen preserves surface articulation and semantic content.
Table 1: Quantitative comparison on two manuscript datasets. Best in bold, second-best underlined. Depth Range Ratio should be read jointly with watertight percentage (see Section 4.3).
